# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for our complete application stack (VPC, EC2, RDS, S3, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure components. We'll add CloudWatch alarms and detailed monitoring after we deploy and verify the basic setup works.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

---
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Our VPC Configuration for a private cloud. A public subnet must be available and not in the same AZ as the default VPC route table (see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_Subnets.html).
Parameters:
  DomainName:
    Description: "The name of our domain."
    Type: String
  EnvironmentType:
    Description: 'Choose Dev, QA or Prod.'
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - dev
      - qa
      - prod
  ManagedPrefixLists: # The names of all the prefix lists that are part of our VPC.
    Description: "Comma separated list with names for all prefix lists in this VPC."
    Type: String
Resources:
  EC2ManagedPrefixList:
    Properties:
        CidrBlocks: [cidr]
      FromPort: 4096 # To avoid conflict with reserved ports
      IpProtocol: tcp
      ToPort: 65535
      PrefixListId: { 'Fn::ImportValue' : prefix-list-id } # This will be set using the AWS CLI
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::PrefixList'

  EC2NetworkAclManagedPrefixListIngressRule:
    Properties:
        CidrBlock: [cidr]
      FromPort: 4096 # To avoid conflict with reserved ports
      IpProtocol: tcp
      ToPort: 65535
      Ipv6CidrBlock: '::/128' # Match the IPv6 CIDR block
      RuleAction: allow # Allow any requests that match this rule to be forwarded
      RuleNumber: [number]
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry'

  EC2NetworkAclManagedPrefixListEgressRule:
    Properties:
        CidrBlock: [cidr]
      FromPort: 4096 # To avoid conflict with reserved ports
      IpProtocol: tcp
      ToPort: 65535
      RuleAction: allow # Allow any requests that match this rule to be forwarded
      RuleNumber: [number]
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry'
